root_text.sn47.2:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati ambapālivane.
root_text.sn47.2:2.4: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.2:2.7: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.2:2.8: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sato hoti.
root_text.sn47.2:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu abhikkante paṭikkante sampajānakārī hoti, ālokite vilokite sampajānakārī hoti, samiñjite pasārite sampajānakārī hoti, saṅghāṭipattacīvaradhāraṇe sampajānakārī hoti, asite pīte khāyite sāyite sampajānakārī hoti, uccārapassāvakamme sampajānakārī hoti, gate ṭhite nisinne sutte jāgarite bhāsite tuṇhībhāve sampajānakārī hoti.
root_text.sn47.2:3.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sampajānakārī hoti.

translation_text.sn47.2:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, in Ambapālī’s mango grove.
translation_text.sn47.2:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.sn47.2:2.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant should live mindful and aware.
translation_text.sn47.2:2.2: This is my instruction to you.
translation_text.sn47.2:2.3: And how is a mendicant mindful?
translation_text.sn47.2:2.4: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.2:2.5: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn47.2:2.7: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.2:2.8: That’s how a mendicant is mindful.
translation_text.sn47.2:3.1: And how is a mendicant aware?
translation_text.sn47.2:3.2: It’s when a mendicant acts with situational awareness when going out and coming back; when looking ahead and aside; when bending and extending the limbs; when bearing the outer robe, bowl and robes; when eating, drinking, chewing, and tasting; when urinating and defecating; when walking, standing, sitting, sleeping, waking, speaking, and keeping silent.
translation_text.sn47.2:3.3: That’s how a mendicant acts with situational awareness.
translation_text.sn47.2:3.4: A mendicant should live mindful and aware.
translation_text.sn47.2:3.5: This is my instruction to you.”
